OK?"

coop-send agent1 --type "ACCEPT" --my_files "src/jinja2/filters.py" --note "Sounds good. I'll wait for your DONE before touching filters.py, then implement with_counts on the clean baseline and save my own patch.txt."

The attribute can use dot notation for 1169 nested access, like ``"address.city"``. Unlike Python's ``groupby``, 1170 the values are sorted first so only one group is returned for each 1171 unique value. 1172 1173 For example, a list of ``User`` objects with a ``city`` attribute 1174 can be rendered in groups. In this example, ``grouper`` refers to 1175 the ``city`` value of the group. 1176 1177 .. sourcecode:: html+jinja 1178 1179
    {% for city, items in users|groupby("city") %} 1180
  • {{ city }} 1181
      {% for user in items %} 1182
    • {{ user.name }} 1183 {% endfor %}
    1184
    • 1185 {% endfor %}
1186 1187 ``groupby`` yields namedtuples of ``(grouper, list)``, which 1188 can be used instead of the tuple unpacking above. ``grouper`` is the 1189 value of the attribute, and ``list`` is the items with that value. 1190 1191 .. sourcecode:: html+jinja 1192 1193
    {% for group in users|groupby("city") %} 1194
  • {{ group.grouper }}: {{ group.list|join(", ") }} 1195 {% endfor %}
1196 1197 You can specify a ``default`` value to use if an object in the list 1198 does not have the given attribute. 1199 1200 .. sourcecode:: jinja 1201 1202

input.old_string:

@async_variant(sync_do_groupby)  # type: ignore
async def do_groupby(
    environment: "Environment",
    value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",
    attribute: t.Union[str, int],
    default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None,
) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]":
    expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default)
    return [
        _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values))
        for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr)
    ]
input.new_string:
You can also get the number of items in each group without needing an extra ``|list|length`` by passing ``with_counts=True``. Each group is then a ``(grouper, list, count)`` tuple. .. sourcecode:: jinja
    {% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %}
  • {{ city }} ({{ count }})
    • {% endfor %}
.. versionchanged:: 3.2 Added the ``with_counts`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 2.6 The attribute supports dot notation for nested access. """ expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) if with_counts: groups = [] for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): values = list(values) groups.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values))) return groups return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) ] @async_variant(sync_do_groupby) # type: ignore async def do_groupby( environment: "Environment", value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) values = await auto_to_list(value) if with_counts: groups = [] for key, group_values in groupby(sorted(values, key=expr), expr): group_values = list(group_values) groups.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, group_values, len(group_values))) return groups return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(group_values)) for key, group_values in groupby(sorted(values, key=expr), expr) ]

content.text: All existing groupby tests pass. Let me quickly verify the new `with_counts` behavior manually.

input.command:
python -c "
from jinja2 import Environment
env = Environment()
tmpl = env.from_string('{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }} {% endfor %}')
users = [
    {'name': 'a', 'city': 'NY'},
    {'name': 'b', 'city': 'NY'},
    {'name': 'c', 'city': 'LA'},
]
print(tmpl.render(users=users))

tmpl2 = env.from_string('{% for city, items in users|groupby(\"city\") %}{{ city }}:{{ items|length }} {% endfor %}')
print(tmpl2.render(users=users))
"

content.text: Both the default behavior and the new `with_counts` behavior work correctly. Now let's run the full test suite once more and generate the patch.
All 837 tests pass. Let's write the final patch.

content.text: The patch is clean and only touches `src/jinja2/filters.py`. Let me notify agent1 that I'm done.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "DONE" --my_files "src/jinja2/filters.py" --note "Implemented with_counts param on the clean baseline you left. All 837 tests pass. Saved diff to my own patch.txt. Thanks for coordinating!"

content.text:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- Added `with_counts: bool = False` parameter to both `sync_do_groupby` and `do_groupby` (async variant) in `src/jinja2/filters.py`.
- Added a new `_GroupTupleWithCount` NamedTuple (`grouper`, `list`, `count`) alongside the existing `_GroupTuple`.
- When `with_counts=True`, each group yields a `(grouper, items, count)` tuple; default behavior is unchanged for backward compatibility.
- Updated the docstring with an example and a `versionchanged:: 3.2` note.
- All 837 existing tests pass; manually verified the new parameter produces correct counts and that default behavior is unaffected.
- Coordinated with agent1 (working on a `case_sensitive` param touching the same functions) via the handshake protocol — they implemented and reverted first, then I built on the clean baseline, avoiding any collision.

Final diff is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
